continents.name: The Absence
continents.biome: Nothing. No light. No air. No ground. You have a body here only because the universe is being polite.

places.name: The Heartbeat Point
places.blurb: The one 'place' in Null where your own heartbeat becomes audible externally. The only sound in the realm.

places.name: The First Heartbeat Monument
places.blurb: An invisible monument marking where the first heartbeat entered Null and grew Ferdinand. Standing here, you feel every heartbeat that has ever happened, all at once. 30-second visitors leave changed. Longer visitors do not leave.

places.name: Smurjan's Seat
places.blurb: A single rock — the only solid object in upper Null. Smurjan uses it when he visits to think. Thinking in Null is different; conclusions arrive without effort.

places.name: The Unwriting
places.blurb: A blank patch where K-XII's existence used to be. Non-enterable because there is nothing to enter.

continents.blurb: The whole of Null is one continent because it is nothing. Subdividing it would require something to subdivide.

continents.name: Ferdinand's Roots
continents.biome: Silver-barked roots descending impossibly far through the absence.

places.name: The Oldest Root
places.blurb: The first root Ferdinand ever grew. It touches the Heartbeat Point. Source of every heartbeat that enters the universe.

places.name: The Root-Spirit Archive
places.blurb: Where retired Root-Spirits gather. Each has a tattooed number of heartbeats counted. Oldest confirmed: 4.8 billion.

places.name: The Silent Root
places.blurb: A root that has stopped drinking. Cause unknown. Ferdinand has not commented. The tree is quieter near it.

places.name: The Forbidden Root
places.blurb: A root sealed with Architect cork. Entering its proximity triggers an Architect response within three heartbeats. Do not approach.

continents.blurb: The only 'continent' in Null that has structure — Ferdinand's silver roots. They are not Null's; they are Ferdinand's, but they are Null's only furniture.

continents.name: The Inscriptions
continents.biome: Non-spatial region where the Seven Sin Codices were etched on Null's 'inside.'

places.name: The Seven Codices Inscription
places.blurb: Where the Seven Sin Codices were written by hands that had to exist before writing existed. Trollmage has read the inscription backwards once. He has not fully returned.

places.name: The Hand That Predates Writing
places.blurb: A preserved imprint in Null's inside wall. A hand. Five fingers. Alien but familiar. Who it belonged to is a cosmic-level secret. Smurjan suspects.

places.name: The Seal Crack
places.blurb: A hairline crack in the cork seal between Null and Scalelist. Monitored by the Architects. Has widened by 0.003 millimeters in the last century.

continents.blurb: The theoretical continent of Null. Where the pre-written things exist. Non-spatial. Non-temporal. Real.

tribes.name: The Void-Worms
tribes.look: Translucent. Long, thin. Visible only in peripheral vision. Eyes like small white pupils.
tribes.customs: Digest absence. Without them, Null would 'clot' — become too dense with nothing. Work in slow, grazing patterns.
tribes.persona: Unintelligent in any conventional sense. Essential. Peaceful. Have never attacked anything in recorded history.

tribes.name: The Root-Spirits
tribes.look: Bark-skinned. Thin. Each has a numeric tattoo on their forearm — their heartbeat count. Silver eyes.
tribes.customs: Attach themselves to a root at birth. Count every heartbeat the root drinks. Pass the count to a successor before dying.
tribes.persona: Patient beyond mortal reckoning. Slow-speaking. Kind to visitors they choose to notice. Most visitors they do not.

tribes.name: The Root-Tenders
tribes.look: Cork-bodied. Hollow eyes — literally, they are carved. Move smoothly. Do not age.
tribes.customs: Tend Ferdinand's roots silently. Never speak. Never eat. Never rest. Servants of the tree itself, not of any Architect.
tribes.persona: Serene. Devoted. Considered by some scholars to be Ferdinand's own remote limbs.

tribes.name: The Codex-Readers of Null
tribes.look: Pale, hollow-eyed. Single-robed. Always bearing a small mirror — they read the Inscriptions by reflection.
tribes.customs: Never look directly at the Inscriptions. Read only by mirror. Leave within three heartbeats of the last line.
tribes.persona: Traumatized. Devout. A few have gone mad; the rest have gone quiet.

tribes.name: The Pre-Writers
tribes.look: Rumored. Theoretical. No confirmed sighting. Smurjan has said he 'has met one.' He will not name them.

characters.name: Smurjan (Null-walker)
characters.role: The only mortal who walks Null casually
characters.portrait_blurb: See /characters/smurjan.png — in Null he appears only in his Fused form. Bare-feet. Tri-colored robe. Carries his own heartbeat as clothing.

characters.name: Elder 4.8 (Root-Spirit)
characters.portrait_blurb: Bark-skinned to the hip. Forearm tattoo: 4,800,000,000. Silver eyes unfocused. Will nod if you name her by number.

characters.name: Root-Spirit One
characters.portrait_blurb: Entirely bark. Features barely visible. Attached to Ferdinand's Oldest Root since the First Heartbeat. Will not speak. Does not need to.

characters.name: Junior Root-Spirit (unassigned)
characters.role: A new Root-Spirit awaiting a root
characters.portrait_blurb: Young. Bark still soft on arms. Tattoo: 0. Waiting.

characters.name: First Tender
characters.portrait_blurb: Cork-bodied, seamless, hollow eye-sockets carved deep. Moves like a thought. Does not speak.

characters.name: Trollmage (visiting)
characters.role: Only living being to have read the Inscription backwards
characters.portrait_blurb: Parts of him remain in Null. When he visits Codexia, witnesses report a faint silver line connecting his shoulder to the void.

characters.name: Reader of the Fifth Line
characters.portrait_blurb: Pale. Carries a small mirror. Reads only at what corresponds to local dusk. Has not dreamed in 20 years.

characters.name: [REDACTED]
characters.role: The hand that wrote the Codex Inscriptions
characters.portrait_blurb: No description permitted. No image known. Bakasura has claimed to have eaten a rumor of them.

fate.threat: Ferdinand's roots withdraw. If Ferdinand ever chose to stop drinking from Null — for whatever cosmic reason — the universe would lose its heartbeat supply. Every HADOM tick would slow, then stop. The Oldest Root is theoretically unmovable, but nothing is theoretically-always in the Scalelist Universe.
fate.consequence: The universe slows to a halt within 12 cosmic cycles. Every realm's HADOM drops to 0. Static existence. Ferdinand becomes an ornament, not an engine. Scholars call this 'the Quiet End.'
fate.prophecy_line: The tree drinks what the void offers. Do not make the tree refuse.
fate.destroyable_by: Only Ferdinand himself, by choice. No attack can sever his roots — they grow back faster than they can be cut. This is why the cosmology is safe: the universe depends on a tree who refuses to stop holding. And Ferdinand has never broken his Oath.

cultural_notes: Null is not 'culture' in any normal sense. It is the cosmological substrate. The 'tribes' listed here are either essential services (Void-Worms, Root-Tenders) or long-term witnesses (Root-Spirits). Visitors to Null are warned: you will hear your own heartbeat externally for the rest of your life if you stay longer than 90 seconds. This is not a curse. It is a permanent awareness that you are alive because Ferdinand's Oldest Root is still drinking.
